title: selaginella setchellii
intro: Selaginella setchellii, also known as the Hawaiian pepper fern, is a species of evergreen, terrestrial fern that belongs to the family Selaginellaceae. It has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Selaginella setchellii is often used in teas, infusions, and salves to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
